target.name: Artimus Robotics
target.founder: Eric Acome, Shane Mitchell, Nick Kellaris, Christoph Keplinger

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Artimus Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $5M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including companies such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and forms the analytical foundation. Using the $5M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied value of $12M. The Peer-Median method was not applied; while seven same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow peer data (fewer than three datapoints at identical or adjacent stages with comparable valuation tiers) precludes reliable median-based triangulation. A broader sector median would introduce unwarranted noise.

The single applicable method supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $14M (high), with a median point of $12M. This range reflects the mechanics of typical seed follow-on rounds but carries material uncertainty given the limited methodological foundation. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable seed-stage robotics transactions and the heterogeneity of business models within the peer set. Additional clarity on Artimus' revenue trajectory, technology differentiation relative to peers, and capital efficiency would improve conviction in this range.
